description: Figure 2 presents a schematic diagram illustrating the conceptual evolution of true stress versus true strain for low-alloy steel under step changes in the Zener-Hollomon parameter (Z_p) at a specific true strain ε_I. The plot is divided into Stage I (before variation) and Stage II (after variation). Four curves are depicted: a black dashed line for constant Z_p1 (lower value), a purple dashed line for constant Z_p2 (higher value, with Z_p2 > Z_p1), a blue solid line for a sudden decrease from Z_p2 to Z_p1 at ε_I, and a red solid line for a sudden increase from Z_p1 to Z_p2 at ε_I. During Stage I, each curve exhibits work hardening followed by dynamic softening, reaching distinct critical strains (ε_c1 < ε_c2 < ε_I) and peak stresses (σ_p2 for the higher Z_p2 path). In Stage II, decreasing Z_p causes an abrupt stress drop toward a new steady-state stress σ_ss1^(v), whereas increasing Z_p produces a stress jump followed by a renewed peak σ_p2^(v) and eventual steady-state stress σ_ss2. This schematic establishes the theoretical framework for modeling multi-stage dynamic recrystallization and transient flow stress responses under varying hot deformation conditions.
